artifact: Report: DirectLLM did not execute data. The SQL counts transactions by calendar month and year from 1994 through 1998, then averages each month-of-year and computes a coefficient of variation. Highest and lowest average_count identify the seasonal peak and trough; lower cv means the seasonal pattern is more stable across years.
sql: WITH ym AS (SELECT EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(date AS DATE)) AS year, EXTRACT(MONTH FROM CAST(date AS DATE)) AS month_num, COUNT(*) AS txn_count FROM trans WHERE EXTRACT(YEAR FROM CAST(date AS DATE)) BETWEEN 1994 AND 1998 GROUP BY 1, 2), stats AS (SELECT month_num, AVG(txn_count) AS avg_count, STDDEV_POP(txn_count) AS sd_count, STDDEV_POP(txn_count) / NULLIF(AVG(txn_count), 0) AS cv FROM ym GROUP BY month_num) SELECT *, avg_count - MIN(avg_count) OVER () AS gap_vs_lowest_month FROM stats ORDER BY avg_count DESC;
